what_it_does: This file is the main indexing orchestrator for the recollection system. It initializes the database and embedding model, scans configured capture-source directories for JSONL conversation transcripts, archives them, parses exchanges, optionally generates per-conversation summaries, computes exchange embeddings, and inserts the results into the database. It exposes full-corpus, single-session, incremental unprocessed, and backfill entry points, and defines a pluggable capture-source registry.
observations: Defines a CaptureSource registry with rows for claude-code, codex, board, and clone.
Enabled file-based sources contribute directories; board and clone are non-file sources with enabled=false and empty dirs().
getCaptureSourceDirs is the single chokepoint that flattens enabled registry rows into sweep directories.
indexConversations archives each JSONL, parses it, optionally summarizes it in batches, then embeds and inserts each exchange.
indexSession locates a single session id across all source directories and indexes only that file.
indexUnprocessed uses a line_end high-water mark from the database to resume append-only JSONL files.
Summary failures write a retryable error sentinel via formatErrorSentinel.
backfillFromSources is now a thin shim that forwards to a streaming driver in backfill.ts.
Module-level side effects set CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS and raise EventEmitter defaultMaxListeners.
Excluded projects and directories are honored via getExcludedProjects and a Set passed to findJsonlFiles.
standouts: Capture-source registry that treats file and non-file sources as enumerable rows.
Line-based high-water mark for incremental resume in indexUnprocessed.
Backfill shim repoints legacy callers to a separate streaming driver.
conventions: Heavy inline commentary explaining design rationale and guide references.
Registry over hard-coded branches for extensibility.
Env-based overrides for test isolation, e.g. TEST_PROJECTS_DIR.
Async batch processing with bounded concurrency via processBatch.
Small pure helpers like existingDirs and sessionIdForSummary.
concerns: indexConversations and indexUnprocessed hold parsed exchanges in memory before embedding, which can grow with large corpora.
backfillFromSources accepts concurrency/noSummaries but ignores them, which may surprise callers.
Module-level mutation of process.env and EventEmitter.defaultMaxListeners is a side effect on import.
summaryPath is derived by a simple .jsonl string replacement, which is fragile if .jsonl appears earlier in the path.
The archive copy in indexUnprocessed runs whenever maxIndexedLine > 0 even if no new lines exist.
No explicit database transaction batching for inserts.
notes: Indexer orchestrates transcript capture, archiving, summarization, embedding, and DB insertion for conversation sources, with incremental resume and a pluggable source registry.
questions: How are board and clone sources actually captured if their registry rows are enabled=false?
What database schema defines line_start and line_end for the high-water mark?
Why does backfillFromSources drop the concurrency and noSummaries arguments?
How does findJsonlFiles identify and handle agent-*.jsonl sidechain files?
Is maxConversations a global limit or per source directory?
What does formatErrorSentinel write and how is it later distinguished from a real summary?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a unified registry-driven capture path for conversation sources, and the code largely matches: it sweeps enabled registry sources, supports incremental resume, and keeps non-file sources enumerable as registry rows. The backfill entry point has been repointed to a separate streaming driver, which is noted rather than hidden.
apparent_themes: Defensive failure handling with retryable sentinels
Archive as a stable processing input
intention_signals: The file is trying to make conversation capture extensible by treating each source as a registry row rather than a hard-coded branch, to avoid reprocessing append-only transcripts via a line-based high-water mark, and to keep the archive/embedding/DB pipeline centralized while delegating large-scale streaming backfill to a dedicated driver.
novelty: Representing non-file sources (board, clone) as registry rows with empty dirs and enabled=false, so source enumeration stays unified even though their capture paths use CLI readers rather than directory sweeps; also the line_end high-water mark for resuming append-only JSONL indexing.
contribution: A capture-source registry abstraction plus multi-mode indexing driver that archives, summarizes, embeds, and persists conversation exchanges, with an incremental resume mechanism and clear separation between file-swept and CLI-driven sources.
summary_for_embedding: Indexer module that orchestrates scanning, archiving, parsing, summarizing, embedding, and database insertion of conversation transcripts from configurable capture sources, with full, session-targeted, incremental, and backfill entry points.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a unified, extensible, and resumable pipeline that turns heterogeneous conversation transcripts into searchable, embedded exchange records.

symbols.does: Interface describing a row in the capture-source registry.
symbols.description: Defines name, harness tag, enabled flag, and a dirs() resolver so each source can declare where its transcripts live. It is the core abstraction that lets file-based and non-file sources coexist as registry rows.

symbols.does: Filters a list of paths to only those that exist on disk.
symbols.description: Small helper used by source dir resolvers to avoid returning non-existent directories to the sweep loop.

symbols.does: Flattens enabled registry rows into the list of directories the indexer walks.
symbols.description: Iterates CAPTURE_SOURCES, skips disabled rows, collects each source's existing dirs, and returns a single flat list used by the indexing entry points. It is the chokepoint that keeps source enumeration centralized.

symbols.does: Runs an async processor over items in fixed-size batches with limited concurrency.
symbols.description: Slices the input array into chunks of the requested size, awaits Promise.all within each chunk, and concatenates results. Used to bound parallel summary generation.

symbols.does: Extracts a session identifier from the first exchange that has one.
symbols.description: Helper that scans a conversation's exchanges to find a sessionId for the summarizer call.

symbols.does: Full-corpus indexing entry point with optional project limit, conversation cap, concurrency, and summary skipping.
symbols.description: Initializes the database and embedding model, walks enabled capture-source directories, skips excluded projects, archives JSONL files, parses conversations, optionally generates summaries in batches, computes per-exchange embeddings, and inserts them into the database until the optional max-conversations limit is reached.

symbols.does: Local type for a conversation queued for summary and embedding in indexConversations.
symbols.description: Bundles the original file path, archive path, summary path, and parsed exchanges so the full-corpus driver can first summarize in parallel and then sequentially embed and insert.

symbols.does: Indexes a single conversation identified by sessionId across all source directories.
symbols.description: Searches source directories for a file whose path contains the session id, archives it, parses and optionally summarizes the exchanges, then embeds and inserts them into the database.

symbols.does: Incremental entry point that resumes indexing from the last recorded line_end per archive.
symbols.description: Collects conversations that have new exchanges beyond the database high-water mark, refreshes archives, optionally summarizes only newly arrived exchanges, and inserts embeddings. It relies on append-only JSONL semantics to avoid reprocessing already-indexed lines.

symbols.does: Local type for a conversation with new exchanges discovered by the incremental driver.
symbols.description: Holds project, file, source/archive/summary paths, and the filtered set of exchanges past the high-water mark.

symbols.does: Compatibility shim that forwards to the streaming backfill driver in backfill.ts.
symbols.description: Preserves the historical name and signature but delegates to backfillConversations, which performs a streaming file-by-file sweep with batched embeddings and checkpoint logging. The concurrency and noSummaries parameters are accepted but no longer drive behavior here.
